  Details:
  Designed and filed the operator-requested one-month follow-up for evolving the Telegram attention watchdog from read-only nudges to drafted responses. Entry specifies: a signal-quality read-out checklist to run at revisit (nudge count, false-positive rate, missed asks, latency improvement); a two-rung rollout where v2a posts context-grounded reply drafts to Saved Messages with sending staying fully manual, and v2b send-on-approval is hard-gated behind comfortable v2a usage plus reply-only scope, per-message approval, send caps, kill switch, and audit log; and the LLM-egress privacy decision that must be made consciously before any code, since the v0 watchdog promises chat content never leaves the box. Trigger ~2026-07-06.
